commonIssues:
A major issue is the failure of thumb impressions on the e-POS (electronic Point of Sale) machine, particularly among daily wage earners and elderly individuals, leading to difficulty in getting rations.

How to fix:

  • Request the dealer to perform the transaction using the Aadhaar-linked mobile OTP method.
  • If persistent, visit the local Rationing Office or BDO office to update your biometrics or request the addition of a 'Nominee' (Proxies) to collect ration on your behalf.

A large number of ration cards (reported as over 1 crore) have been deactivated due to failure to link Aadhaar or failure in biometric verification.

How to fix:

  • Check the status of your card on the Food and Supplies Department portal (food.wb.gov.in).
  • If deactivated, complete the Aadhaar linking process through the portal, mobile app ('Khadya Sathi'), or by visiting your local Rationing Office with your Aadhaar card and digital ration card.

Many, particularly in regions like the Darjeeling Hills, reported massive errors in names, addresses, and photographs in the newly digitized ration cards.

How to fix:

  • Apply for data correction using Form 5 (for change of name/address/other details) online via the Food and Supplies portal.
  • Ensure the data matches your Aadhaar card exactly to avoid further rejection.

Mismatch between demographic data (name, spelling, date of birth) in the Aadhaar database and the ration card database causes significant delays and rejection of updates.

How to fix:

  • First, ensure your Aadhaar details are correct. If not, update Aadhaar first at a permanent enrollment center.
  • Then, use the corrected Aadhaar to update the Ration Card details through the West Bengal Food Department's 'Aadhaar-Seeding' portal.

Reports of dealers providing lower quantities of foodgrains than the entitlement or demanding extra money.

How to fix:

  • Check the official price and quantity list displayed at the Fair Price Shop (FPS).
  • Lodge an immediate complaint by calling the toll-free numbers 1967 or 1800-345-5505 (from 8 AM to 8 PM).

The e-POS machines sometimes fail due to poor internet connectivity, especially in rural areas, delaying distribution.

How to fix:

  • The state allows 'Manual Distribution' under specific conditions (network outage documented by the Inspector). If the machine fails repeatedly, ask the dealer for the manual register entry provision.
  • Wait for a few hours and try again, as server bandwidth is usually restored during non-peak hours.

Confusion over changing quotas (e.g., changes from wheat to rice) and the expiration of entitlements.

How to fix:

  • Follow the local news and the Food and Supplies Department's official social media pages for announcements regarding quota changes.
  • Verify your specific category (AAY, PHH, RKSY-I, RKSY-II) and its current allocation on the 'Check Entitlement' section of the official website.
